Summer Program (2025) Old page
Visit new page:
https://ds4all.ics.uci.edu/summer2025/

Summer Program (2025)
07/07/2025 – 07/11/2025,
UCI ISEB 1330
This NSF-funded summer program teaches students the principles of data science and machine learning. Students will learn concepts about data modeling, data cleaning, data wrangling, and visualization. Students will learn basic Python programming for processing data and learn ML techniques, including basic concepts, training, classification, and sentiment analysis.
The program will use the open-source
Texera
platform to help students get familiar with these concepts even if they have a limited computing background. We will include a capstone project for students to learn these skills by analyzing real data (e.g., social media data) to apply the knowledge to conduct ML-based data science. The instructors and staff include professors and Ph.D. students from UCI and UCLA who are experts in data management, data science, AI, and machine learning. A gallery showcasing the classroom and learning environment for the program is available.
Program Photos
Classroom Photos
Students who complete the program without any absence will receive a certificate from the program.

The Data Science for All (DS4ALL) program teaches students fundamental principles of data science, artificial intelligence (AI), and machine learning (ML), in a “no code and low code” experience. Students learn core concepts such as data modeling, cleaning, wrangling, and visualization. Students also learn foundational AI/ML techniques, including model training, classification, and sentiment analysis. The program leverages the open-source
Apache Texera
(Incubating) platform to make these concepts accessible to students with limited computing backgrounds.
Summer 2026 Program for High-School Students
for is available!
High School Summer Programs
Summers of 2023 – 2025: Teaching high-school students data science and AI/ML. The experience of this program was presented and shared at

conference (
DSE-K12 2025
), held from February 17-19 in San Antonio, TX.

Summer Program (2023)
This NSF-funded summer program teaches students the principles of data science and machine learning. Students will learn concepts about data modeling, databases, SQL, data cleaning, data wrangling, and visualization. Students will learn basic Python programming for processing data and learn machine learning techniques, including basic concepts, training, classification, and sentiment analysis.
The program will use the open-source Texera platform to help students get familiar with these concepts even if they have a limited computing background. We will include a capstone project for students to learn these skills by analyzing real data (e.g., social media data) to apply the knowledge to conduct ML-based data science. The instructors and staff include professors and Ph.D. students from UCI and UCLA who are experts in data management, data science, and machine learning.

Summer Program (2024)
This NSF-funded summer program teaches students the principles of data science and machine learning. Students will learn concepts about data modeling, data cleaning, data wrangling, and visualization. Students will learn basic Python programming for processing data and learn ML techniques, including basic concepts, training, classification, and sentiment analysis.
The program will use the open-source Texera platform to help students get familiar with these concepts even if they have a limited computing background. We will include a capstone project for students to learn these skills by analyzing real data (e.g., social media data) to apply the knowledge to conduct ML-based data science. The instructors and staff include professors and Ph.D. students from UCI and UCLA who are experts in data management, data science, and machine learning.

Data Science for All Summer Program for High Schoolers (2026)
Learning data science and AI/ML with no code and low code
July 6-10, 2026,
ISEB Building
, UC Irvine
This DS4ALL summer program teaches high-school students the principles of data science and machine learning. Students will learn concepts about data modeling, data cleaning, data wrangling, and visualization. Students will learn basic Python programming for processing data and get familiar with AI, and machine learning (ML) techniques.
This is the 4th DS4ALL summer program. Information about earlier offerings is available
here
.
The program will use the open-source Apache
Texera
(Incubating) platform to help students get familiar with these concepts even if they have a limited computing background. We will include a capstone project for students to learn these skills by analyzing real data to apply the knowledge to conduct ML-based data science with a “no code and low code” experience. The organizers, instructors, and staff include professors and Ph.D. students from UCI and UCLA, who are experts in data management, data science, AI, and ML. Students who complete the program without any absence will receive a certificate from the program. Students with good performance are eligible for recommendation letters from the organizers.
Seats are limited.

Prerequisites:
Algebra II or Integrated Math II

Data Science for All (Old Page)
This NSF-funded summer program teaches students the principles of data science and machine learning. Students will learn concepts about data modeling, data cleaning, data wrangling, and visualization. Students will learn basic Python programming for processing data and learn ML techniques, including basic concepts, training, classification, and sentiment analysis.
The program uses the open-source Texera platform to help students get familiar with these concepts even if they have a limited computing background. We include a capstone project for students to learn these skills by analyzing real data (e.g., social media data) to apply the knowledge to conduct ML-based data science. The instructors and staff include professors and Ph.D. students from UCI and UCLA who are experts in data management, data science, and machine learning.
We had a successful program in 2023, 2024, and 2025! Click the below for more details:
